title: Cloud Engineer
description: Cloud Engineers design, deploy, and manage an organization's cloud computing infrastructure. They focus on maintaining high availability, optimizing costs, building automation pipelines, and configuring virtual networks.

topics.name: Linux Admin & Virtualization
topics.description: Learn terminal commands, shell scripting, package management, SSH keys, and how hypervisors run virtual machines.

topics.name: Networking in the Cloud
topics.description: Understand IP subnets, CIDR blocks, DNS, firewalls, and routing tables, which are critical for virtual private clouds.

topics.name: Git & Code Collaboration
topics.description: Master branching, committing, pull requests, and standard Git commands to manage infrastructure code.

topics.name: Core AWS Services (or Azure/GCP)
topics.description: Get familiar with Virtual Servers (EC2), Object Storage (S3), Identity & Access Management (IAM), and Virtual Networks (VPC).

topics.name: Cloud Storage & Database Options
topics.description: Learn differences between block storage, file systems, and object storage. Study relational RDS vs NoSQL DynamoDB.

topics.name: Basic Cloud Security
topics.description: Understand the shared responsibility model, IAM policies, least privilege principle, and security groups.

topics.name: Containerization with Docker
topics.description: Write Dockerfiles, build images, run containers, mount volumes, and expose ports to host applications.

topics.name: Infrastructure as Code (Terraform)
topics.description: Use HashiCorp Configuration Language (HCL) to provision, modify, and destroy cloud infrastructure automatically.

topics.name: CI/CD & Automation
topics.description: Build deployment pipelines using GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or Jenkins to test and deploy applications automatically.

topics.name: Container Orchestration (Kubernetes)
topics.description: Learn Kubernetes architecture: Pods, Services, Deployments, ConfigMaps, and how to manage container clusters.

topics.name: Serverless Computing
topics.description: Build event-driven applications using AWS Lambda, Azure Functions, or Google Cloud Functions without managing servers.

topics.name: Monitoring & Observability
topics.description: Set up monitoring dashboards and alerts for system metrics and application logs using Prometheus, Grafana, and CloudWatch.
